title: The New PM, Claire Vo | Compile 26
summary: Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, discusses how product management is fundamentally changing in an era where anyone can build software but market discovery and customer demand are the scarce resources. The talk explores how PM strategy must shift from managing engineering constraints to identifying genuine customer needs and manifesting novel ideas in a market-abundant but product-scarce environment.
writeup: Claire Vo, founder of ChatPRD, argues that as code abundance makes building technically easy, the product manager role shifts away from execution and toward finding market fit and articulating novel ideas. The core claim is that the binding constraint has moved from engineering capacity to identifying what users actually want. The talk connects this shift to cost management, product-market fit, and idea generation as the new center of PM work.
